sections.html:
Regional Style · Japan

Japanese Web Design

Dense grids of information, tiny text packed into every available pixel, bright red accent labels, and the methodical organization of a culture that values thoroughness over minimalism — Japanese Web Design reflects the unique information architecture of Japan's commercial internet. Where Western design removes, Japanese design adds: more links, more sections, more content, all organized in a surprisingly navigable grid system.

This information-dense style dominates Japanese e-commerce giants, newspaper sites, and corporate portals. You'll see it on railway company sites, department store pages, electronics retailers, and municipal government portals — anywhere that serves a Japanese audience accustomed to scanning dense, content-rich layouts efficiently.
